docs.title: GlobeTalk Documentation 🌍✉️
docs.text: Welcome to the official documentation for GlobeTalk, a virtual pen pal platform that connects people worldwide for text-based cultural exchange and global friendships. This site provides comprehensive guides for users and developers, covering features, APIs, UI, setup, and more.

docs.title: About GlobeTalk
docs.text:
GlobeTalk simulates the traditional pen pal experience through modern digital messaging, emphasizing curiosity, connection, and global diversity. Users can match with random pen pals, exchange delayed text messages, explore cultural profiles, and engage safely with moderated content.

Target Users:

  • Language learners
  • Cultural exchange enthusiasts
  • Students and educators

Travelers seeking global connections

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Explore Features to understand what GlobeTalk offers.
  2. Set Up GlobeTalk to run the project locally.
  3. Learn to Use GlobeTalk with step-by-step guides.
  4. Dive into APIs for developers integrating with GlobeTalk.
  5. Contribute to the project with our guidelines.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: Have questions or feedback? Open an issue on our GitHub repository or contact the team at globetalk92@gmail.com

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: We welcome contributions to GlobeTalk! Follow these guidelines to contribute effectively.

docs.title: Branching Strategy
docs.text:
• Main Branch (main): Stable, production-ready code.
  • Testing Branch (testing): For integrating and validating features.
  • Feature Branches (feature/xyz): For new features or bug fixes.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create a Feature Branch
  2. Name: feature/<short-description>
  3. Base: testing

  4. Example: git checkout -b feature/add-profile-endpoint testing

  5. Implement and Commit

  6. Use clear commit messages: type(scope): message

  7. Example: feat(api): add profile update endpoint

  8. Push to GitHub

  9. Push your feature branch: git push origin feature/add-profile-endpoint

  10. Create a Pull Request (PR)

  11. Base: testing
  12. Compare: Your feature branch

  13. Include a descriptive title and testing notes.

  14. Code Review

  15. At least one reviewer must approve.

  16. Reviewers check code quality, functionality, and no breaking changes.

  17. Testing

  18. Run tests locally: npm test
  19. Ensure 80% code coverage with Jest.

  20. CI pipeline runs unit and integration tests.

  21. Merging

  22. Merge feature to testing after approval and CI checks.
  23. Merge testing to main after validation.
  24. Delete merged feature branches.

docs.title: Scrum Methodology
docs.text:
GlobeTalk uses Scrum for agile development across four sprints:

  • Sprint Planning: Select features and tasks for each sprint.
  • Daily Stand-ups: Discuss progress and blockers (15 minutes daily).
  • Sprint Review: Demonstrate features to stakeholders.
  • Sprint Retrospective: Reflect on what worked and what to improve.

docs.title: Team Roles
docs.text:
• Product Owner: Shaneel Kassen (defines vision, prioritizes features)
  • Scrum Master: Paballo Lipopo (facilitates events, removes obstacles)
  • Development Team: Nfundwenhle Ezra Nkontwana, Kuhlula Mambane, Tiyani Mabunda, Bongani Nobela, Paballo Lipopo

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository.
  2. Clone your fork: git clone https://github.com/your-username/globetalk.git
  3. Follow Installation to set up the project.
  4. Create a feature branch and start coding!

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Use ESLint for JavaScript: npx eslint .
  • Follow Firebase best practices for Functions and Firestore.

docs.title: Feedback
docs.text: Report issues or suggest improvements via GitHub Issues.

docs.title: Database Components
docs.text: GlobeTalk uses Firebase Firestore for scalable, real-time data storage. Below are the main collections and their schemas.

docs.title: User Profiles
docs.text:
Stores user preferences and cultural information.

  • Collection: users
  • Document: <userId>
  • Fields: 
    {
  "region": "string", // e.g., "Asia"
  "hobbies": ["string"], // e.g., ["music", "cooking"]
  "languages": ["string"], // e.g., ["English", "Mandarin"]
  "bio": "string", // Max 200 characters
  "createdAt": "timestamp"
}

docs.title: Match Records
docs.text:
Tracks user pairings.

  • Collection: matches
  • Document: <matchId>
  • Fields: 
    {
  "user1Id": "string",
  "user2Id": "string",
  "createdAt": "timestamp",
  "oneTime": boolean
}

docs.title: Message Storage
docs.text:
Stores messages with delivery delays.

  • Collection: messages
  • Document: <messageId>
  • Fields: 
    {
  "matchId": "string",
  "senderId": "string",
  "content": "string", // Max 1000 characters
  "emojis": ["string"],
  "queuedAt": "timestamp",
  "deliveryAt": "timestamp"
}

docs.title: Moderation Logs
docs.text:
Tracks reports and bans.

  • Collection: moderation
  • Document: <reportId>
  • Fields: 
    {
  "userId": "string", // Optional
  "messageId": "string", // Optional
  "reason": "string",
  "status": "string", // e.g., "pending", "resolved"
  "createdAt": "timestamp"
}

docs.title: Features
docs.text: GlobeTalk offers a rich set of features to foster cultural exchange and global connections through text-based messaging.

docs.title: Random Matchmaking
docs.text:
• Connect with a random pen pal from another country based on time zone or language preferences.
  • Choose between one-time messages or ongoing correspondence.
  • Example: Select "Spanish" as a preferred language to match with a pen pal from Spain or Latin America.

docs.title: Asynchronous Messaging
docs.text:
• Send and receive text-based letters with a 12-hour delivery delay to mimic postal mail.
  • Messages support basic emojis for expressive communication.
  • Messages are stored privately and accessible only to matched users.

docs.title: Cultural Profiles
docs.text:
• Create a short intro about yourself (e.g., age range, hobbies, region).
  • View anonymous facts about your pen pal, such as regional holidays or sayings.
  • Example: Learn that your pen pal from Japan celebrates Obon, a festival honoring ancestors.

docs.title: Safety & Moderation
docs.text:
• Anonymous chats with no personal identifiers (names, emails).
  • Flag inappropriate content or block users for a safe experience.
  • Text-only messaging (no media or file sharing) to reduce risks.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Follow these steps to set up GlobeTalk locally for development.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js (v16 or higher)
  • npm
  • Firebase CLI
  • Git

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Clone the Repository 

    git clone https://github.com/example/globetalk.git
cd globetalk

  2. Initialize npm 

    npm init -y

  3. Install Dependencies 

    npm install eslint --save-dev
npx eslint --init
npm install jest --save-dev

  4. Set Up Firebase

  5. Install Firebase CLI: 
    npm install -g firebase-tools
  6. Log in to Firebase: 
    firebase login
  7. Initialize Firebase in the project: 
    firebase init

  8. Select Firestore, Functions, and Hosting, then follow the prompts.

  9. Run Locally

  10. Start the development server: 
    firebase emulators:start
  11. Open http://localhost:5000 in your browser.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Ensure Firebase configuration (firebase.json) is correct.
  • Check that Firestore security rules allow read/write access for testing.

docs.title: UI Modules
docs.text: GlobeTalk's user interface is built with HTML, CSS, and Vanilla JavaScript, designed for simplicity and responsiveness.

docs.title: Match Screen
docs.text:
• Displays options to find a new pen pal with filters (language, time zone, one-time vs. ongoing).
  • Example: Select "French" and "UTC+1" to match with a user from France.

docs.title: Message Inbox
docs.text:
• Shows active conversations with pen pals.
  • Displays message status (queued, delivered) and timestamps.
  • Example: View a thread with a pen pal from Japan, showing delayed messages.

docs.title: Compose Letter
docs.text:
• A text editor for writing messages with emoji support.
  • Shows a preview of the message and delivery delay (12 hours).
  • Example: Write "Greetings from Canada! 🍁" and schedule delivery.

docs.title: Cultural Explorer
docs.text:
• Displays region-based facts about your pen pal (e.g., holidays, sayings).
  • Example: Learn about Brazil’s Carnival or a Swedish Midsummer tradition.

docs.title: Settings & Safety
docs.text:
• Adjust preferences (e.g., language, notifications).
  • Block or report users and view app policies.
  • Example: Block a user or report a message for inappropriate content.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: Learn how to use GlobeTalk as a user or developer.

docs.title: For Users
docs.text:
1. Sign Up
  2. Create an account using Firebase Authentication (Google).

  3. Set up your profile with region, hobbies, and languages.

  4. Find a Pen Pal

  5. Go to the Match Screen and select preferences (e.g., language: Spanish, time zone: UTC-3).
  6. Choose one-time or ongoing correspondence.

  7. Example: Match with a user from Argentina for ongoing chats.

  8. Send a Message

  9. In the Compose Letter section, write a message (e.g., "Hello! What's your favorite holiday? 😊").

  10. Submit to queue it for delivery in 12 hours.

  11. Explore Cultural Facts

  12. Visit the Cultural Explorer to view facts about your pen pal’s region.

  13. Example: Learn about Diwali if matched with an Indian user.

  14. Stay Safe

  15. Use the Settings & Safety section to block or report users.
  16. Example: Report a message for inappropriate content.

docs.title: For Developers
docs.text:
1. API Integration
  2. Authenticate with Firebase to get a token.
  3. Use the Matchmaking API to connect users.

  4. Example: Send a POST /match request to pair a user with a Spanish-speaking pen pal.

  5. Local Testing

  6. Run Firebase emulators to test API endpoints locally.

  7. Use Postman to send requests to http://localhost:5001/globetalk/us-central1/api.

  8. Database Access

  9. Query the users collection to retrieve profiles.
  10. Example: Fetch a pen pal’s profile with GET /profile/<userId>.

docs.title: Example Workflow
docs.text:
1. Sign up and set your profile to "Region: Europe, Language: French."
  2. Match with a pen pal from France.
  3. Send a message: "Bonjour! Tell me about French culture! 🇫🇷"
  4. After 12 hours, receive a reply and view their cultural fact about Bastille Day.

docs.title: API Overview
docs.text: GlobeTalk provides a set of RESTful APIs to manage matchmaking, messaging, user profiles, and moderation. These APIs are built with Firebase Functions and integrate with Firebase Firestore for data storage.

docs.title: API Modules
docs.text:
• Matchmaking API: Connect users with available pen pals based on filters like language or time zone.
  • Message API: Handle sending, delaying, and retrieving text-based messages.
  • Profile API: Manage user cultural profiles, including region and hobbies.
  • Moderation API: Report users, manage bans, and review flagged content.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: All APIs require Firebase Authentication tokens. Include the token in the Authorization header as Bearer <token>.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: https://us-central1-globetalk.cloudfunctions.net/api

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Set up Firebase Authentication for your app.
  2. Obtain an access token for authenticated requests.
  3. Use the endpoints below to interact with GlobeTalk's features.

docs.title: Matchmaking API
docs.text: The Matchmaking API connects users with random pen pals based on filters like language or time zone.

docs.title: POST /match
docs.text: Matches the authenticated user with a new pen pal.

docs.title: Request
docs.text:
• Headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>
  • Body: 
    {
  "language": "string", // Optional: e.g., "Spanish"
  "timezone": "string", // Optional: e.g., "UTC+1"
  "oneTime": boolean // true for one-time message, false for ongoing
}

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: 
    {
  "matchId": "string",
  "penPal": {
    "userId": "string",
    "region": "string",
    "language": "string"
  }
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -X POST https://us-central1-globetalk.cloudfunctions.net/api/match \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"language": "Spanish", "oneTime": false}'

docs.title: Message API
docs.text: The Message API handles sending, delaying, and retrieving text-based messages between matched users.

docs.title: POST /messages
docs.text: Sends a new message with a 12-hour delivery delay.

docs.title: Request
docs.text:
• Headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>
  • Body: 
    {
  "matchId": "string",
  "content": "string", // Text message, max 1000 characters
  "emojis": ["string"] // Optional: Array of emoji codes
}

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 201 Created
  • Body: 
    {
  "messageId": "string",
  "status": "queued",
  "deliveryTime": "ISO8601 timestamp"
}

docs.title: GET /messages/{matchId}
docs.text: Retrieves all messages for a match.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: 
    [
  {
    "messageId": "string",
    "content": "string",
    "senderId": "string",
    "timestamp": "ISO8601 timestamp"
  }
]

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -X POST https://us-central1-globetalk.cloudfunctions.net/api/messages \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"matchId": "abc123", "content": "Hello from Brazil! 😊"}'

docs.title: Moderation API
docs.text: The Moderation API handles reporting users, managing bans, and reviewing flagged content.

docs.title: POST /report
docs.text: Reports a message or user for inappropriate content.

docs.title: Request
docs.text:
• Headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>
  • Body: 
    {
  "messageId": "string", // Optional
  "userId": "string", // Optional
  "reason": "string" // e.g., "Inappropriate language"
}

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 201 Created
  • Body: 
    {
  "reportId": "string",
  "status": "submitted"
}

docs.title: POST /block
docs.text: Blocks a user to prevent further communication.

docs.title: Request
docs.text:
• Headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>
  • Body: 
    {
  "userId": "string"
}

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: 
    {
  "blocked": true
}

docs.title: Profile API
docs.text: The Profile API manages user cultural profiles, including region, hobbies, and languages.

docs.title: PUT /profile
docs.text: Updates the authenticated user's profile.

docs.title: Request
docs.text:
• Headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>
  • Body: 
    {
  "region": "string", // e.g., "South America"
  "hobbies": ["string"], // e.g., ["reading", "travel"]
  "languages": ["string"], // e.g., ["English", "Portuguese"]
  "bio": "string" // Max 200 characters
}

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: 
    {
  "userId": "string",
  "updated": true
}

docs.title: GET /profile/{userId}
docs.text: Retrieves a pen pal's anonymous profile.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: 
    {
  "region": "string",
  "hobbies": ["string"],
  "languages": ["string"],
  "bio": "string",
  "funFact": "string" // e.g., "Celebrates Diwali"
}
